profile.name: Napat Kongsawad
profile.bio: Multimedia journalist based in Bangkok, experienced in leading coverage for an international audience. Politics, business, and compelling stories.

sections.title: NHK World
sections.note: Broadcast packages and reporting for NHK WORLD-JAPAN.

sections.title: Associated Press
sections.note: Wire reporting from Bangkok for a global audience.

sections.title: Bloomberg
sections.note: Markets, commodities, and Southeast Asian politics.

clips.title: Myanmar joins ASEAN foreign ministers meeting
clips.summary: Myanmar's top diplomat joins the ASEAN foreign ministers' meeting, testing the bloc's approach to the junta after its election.

clips.title: Thai police detain Japanese leader of scam group based in Cambodia
clips.summary: Thai police detain a Japanese national accused of leading a Cambodia-based scam operation, part of the widening regional crackdown on fraud networks.

clips.title: Resurfaced Death Railway offers rare glimpse into dark history
clips.summary: A resurfaced section of the WWII-era Death Railway offers a rare glimpse into one of the darkest chapters of Thailand's wartime history.

clips.title: Thai foreign minister visits Myanmar
clips.summary: Coverage of the Thai foreign minister's visit to post-election Myanmar and what it signals for Bangkok's role as a broker with the junta.

clips.title: Thailand's fans of washoku crave variety
clips.summary: How Thai diners' appetite for Japanese food has grown far beyond sushi — and how restaurants are scrambling to keep up with demand for variety.

clips.title: Thailand's Death Fest event encourages end-of-life planning
clips.summary: Inside Bangkok's Death Fest, where a rapidly aging Thailand confronts mortality through end-of-life planning workshops and death-positive events.

clips.title: 20 Thai crew repatriated after cargo ship attacked near Strait of Hormuz
clips.summary: Twenty Thai crew members arrive home after their cargo ship was attacked near the Strait of Hormuz, caught in the region's shipping crisis.

clips.title: FM Sihasak hopeful for democracy in Myanmar after election
clips.summary: Thailand's foreign minister voices cautious hope for Myanmar's democratic transition in an interview following the country's election.

clips.title: 20 years on, Thai abductee's family still awaiting answers from North Korea
clips.summary: Two decades after Anocha Panjoy vanished into North Korea's abduction program, her family is still pressing Pyongyang for answers.

clips.title: Thais anxious over Myanmar rare earths
clips.summary: An investigation tracking the Myanmar–China rare-earth supply chain — a five-fold export surge and its toxic fallout on Mekong communities downstream.

clips.title: Thai police detain two Japanese possibly over Myanmar scam hub case
clips.summary: Thai police detain two Japanese men linked to a major scam operation in eastern Myanmar, held on immigration charges as the investigation widens.

clips.title: Thai police: Myanmar scam centers moving to Cambodia, Laos
clips.summary: Royal Thai Police Senior Inspector-General Thatchai Pitaneelaboot tells NHK that crackdowns are pushing scam groups out of Myanmar into Cambodia and Laos.

clips.title: International victims caught up in scam centers along Thai-Myanmar borderlands
clips.summary: First-person testimony from international victims trafficked into scam compounds along the Thai–Myanmar borderlands.

clips.title: Bangladeshi who escaped Myanmar scam center recounts his entrapment
clips.summary: A 25-year-old Bangladeshi survivor recounts how a broker's job offer in Thailand ended in captivity inside a Myanmar scam compound — and how he escaped.

clips.title: Trump's foreign aid pause suspends removal of US-dropped bombs in Vietnam, Laos
clips.summary: Washington's foreign-aid freeze halts the clearance of unexploded American ordnance still killing people in Laos and Vietnam.

clips.title: Foreigners forced to work in Myanmar-based fraud rings
clips.summary: How foreigners are lured to Myanmar-based fraud compounds and forced to run online scams — reported through survivor accounts.

clips.title: After the king's endorsement, Thailand has a new Cabinet but with some familiar faces
clips.summary: Thailand seats a new cabinet under PM Paetongtarn Shinawatra, mixing fresh faces with familiar names from the political dynasties.

clips.title: Thaksin's daughter Paetongtarn Shinawatra is elected Thailand's prime minister
clips.summary: Parliament elects Thaksin's youngest daughter Paetongtarn Shinawatra as Thailand's youngest-ever prime minister, extending the Shinawatra dynasty.

clips.title: Thai courts that have disbanded multiple governments are accused of setting back democracy
clips.summary: Analysis of Thailand's Constitutional Court and its record of dissolving elected governments — and what that means for Thai democracy.

clips.title: Traces of cyanide are found in the blood of Vietnamese and Americans found dead in a Bangkok hotel
clips.summary: Six people found dead in a Bangkok Grand Hyatt suite were poisoned with cyanide, forensic tests confirm, in a case that gripped the region.

clips.title: WikiLeaks' Assange pleads guilty to publishing US military secrets in deal that secures his freedom
clips.summary: Julian Assange pleads guilty in a Saipan courtroom in a deal with the US Justice Department, ending a years-long legal saga and securing his freedom.

clips.title: Rights groups urge Thailand not to extradite Vietnamese activist, saying he's at risk if sent home
clips.summary: Rights groups urge Thailand not to extradite a Vietnamese activist who they warn faces persecution if sent home.

clips.title: The Constitutional Court of Thailand agrees to hear a case that could imperil the prime minister
clips.summary: The Constitutional Court accepts a petition that could unseat PM Srettha Thavisin over a controversial cabinet appointment.

clips.title: 'Sheer terror': Passengers describe turbulence-hit flight that put 20 in intensive care
clips.summary: Survivors describe the seconds of violent turbulence that killed one passenger and put twenty in intensive care.

clips.title: Most of passengers from battered Singapore Airlines jetliner arrive in Singapore from Bangkok
clips.summary: Passengers from the turbulence-stricken Singapore Airlines flight reach Singapore after the emergency landing in Bangkok.

clips.title: A monarchy reform activist in Thailand dies in detention after a monthslong hunger strike
clips.summary: Monarchy-reform activist Netiporn 'Bung' Sanesangkhom dies in detention after a months-long hunger strike, reigniting debate over lèse-majesté detentions.

clips.title: Rice surges to three-year high as Indian export ban roils market
clips.summary: Asian rice prices hit a three-year high after India bans exports, stoking food-security fears across the region.

clips.title: Rice prices to rise on India export curbs, adding to Ukraine, weather woes
clips.summary: India's export curbs threaten to push Asian rice prices toward a decade high, compounding pressures from war and weather.

clips.title: Cambodian PM walks back on Pita defeat post after Twitter storm
clips.summary: Cambodia's Hun Sen walks back a social-media post gloating over Pita's defeat after an online backlash.

clips.title: Global supplies of sugar, rice at risk from looming Thai drought
clips.summary: A looming El Niño drought in Thailand threatens global supplies of sugar and rice.

clips.title: Thai coalition settles Speaker row as prime minister vote looms
clips.summary: Thailand's election-winning coalition resolves its speaker dispute ahead of the prime-ministerial vote.

clips.title: Thailand destroys $600 million worth of seized meth, cocaine
clips.summary: Thailand incinerates $600 million worth of seized methamphetamine, heroin and cocaine in a show of force against traffickers.

clips.title: World's rice stockpiles will be put to the test with El Niño's return
clips.summary: With El Niño returning, the world's rice stockpiles face their first real stress test in years.

clips.title: Dry weather to slash Australia's next wheat crop by a third
clips.summary: Dry weather is set to cut Australia's next wheat crop by a third, tightening global grain supplies.

clips.title: Binance CEO sees Bitcoin soaring to $80,000 this year
clips.summary: Binance's CEO predicts Bitcoin will hit $80,000 as the crypto market rebounds from its long winter.

clips.title: Increase in northern Thai ship traffic to China is coming: Official
clips.summary: Officials expect a surge in cargo traffic from northern Thailand to China through the Mekong's Chiang Saen Port.

clips.title: Thaksin's homecoming: Parole debate and political fallout
clips.summary: Thaksin's return from exile ignites a parole debate and reshuffles Thailand's political landscape.

clips.title: Land bridge project gets the nod from Parliament despite concerns
clips.summary: Parliament endorses the Southern land-bridge megaproject despite environmental and cost concerns.

clips.title: Police arrest journalist, photographer over coverage of graffiti in March 2023
clips.summary: Police arrest a journalist and a photographer over their coverage of protest graffiti, raising press-freedom alarms in Thailand.

clips.title: Bank of Thailand holds key interest rate at 2.50% in a 5-2 decision
clips.summary: The Bank of Thailand resists government pressure for cuts and holds its key rate at 2.50% in a split 5–2 decision.

clips.title: Phang Nga airport feasibility study back on track in bid to boost tourism in South
clips.summary: A revived feasibility study for a Phang Nga airport aims to unlock tourism on the Andaman coast.

clips.title: Lawmakers accept unanimously all 7 drafts of Clean Air Bill
clips.summary: Lawmakers unanimously accept all seven drafts of the Clean Air Bill, a milestone in the fight against seasonal haze.

topics.note: The coup's fallout, from diplomacy to supply chains.

topics.note: Wire-speed coverage of the region's biggest stories.
